## Review — T3.1 slice 8 (Metal `seglu`)

**Scope:** Slice 8 only (`seglu` F32/F16/BF16). Roadmap **T3.1** stays unchecked until full Metal `device.Backend` is done.

### Deliverables (working tree)

| Artifact | Evidence |
|----------|----------|
| Metal kernel | `activation_seglu.metal` — `seglu_float4` = `up * metal_fast_sigmoid32_float4(gate)` (`:56-58`), aligned with `FastSeGLU32` in `pkg/backend/device/cpu/math/extra_activation.go:88-90` |
| Registry | `activation_seglu.go` — `kernels.Default` `"seglu"`, F32/F16/BF16, `runMetalSeGLUKernel` |
| Device path | `activation_seglu_darwin.go` → `C.metal_dispatch_seglu`; stub for non-darwin |
| Bridge | `bridge_utility_darwin.m:564-612` — `metal_utility_dispatch`, kernel names `seglu_float32` / `float16` / `bfloat16`; `bridge_darwin.h:1233` declaration |
| Parity reference | `activation_seglu_expected_test.go:19,35` — **`cpuactivation.SeGLUTensorsF32Generic`** only (acceptance §11) |
| Tests / bench | `activation_seglu_test.go` — `parityElementCounts` (`backend_test.go:17` = {1,7,64,1024,8192}), `segluMaxULP: 2` |
| `kernels.metallib` | Binary updated in WT (regenerated with new `.metal`) |

**Math vs SiGLU:** SiGLU metal uses `sigmoid(gate) * up` (`activation_siglu.metal:56-58` / `FastSiGLU32`). SeGLU uses `up * sigmoid(gate)` per `FastSeGLU32` and `SeGLUTensorsF32Generic` (`gated_f32_generic.go:59-66`). Operand order matches the CPU generic, not SiGLU’s.

**Tails:** F32 remainder uses `seglu_write_tail` (`:69-77`, `:116`). F16/BF16 tails write only `tail` lanes in epilogue loops (same pattern as slices 3–7).

**Hot path:** Full-vector `float4` multiply; per-lane `metal_fast_sigmoid32` inside `metal_fast_sigmoid32_float4` — same accepted Metal gated-activation pattern as `siglu`/`glu` (cycles 62–66). No host-side masquerading; no banned phrasing in seglu files.

### Regression (steering)

Ran on arm64: SeGLU + siglu, reglu, glu, geglu, swiglu, gelu, steer, graft — all pass.

### Root cause
